title: pyrus aria subsp. intermedia
intro: Pyrus aria subsp. intermedia, also known as wild service tree, is a deciduous tree native to Europe and Western Asia. Its bark and leaves have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The medicinal properties of Pyrus aria subsp. intermedia are believed to provide relief from various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, due to its ability to soothe and protect tissues. The plant is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion for its medicinal uses, and is also used in the production of jams and preserves.
